safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_tg short entry $66,619 at the 12H supply; reaffirmed zero_live 2026-06-22 as the key reload level (~66.3 flip). Higher/aggressive reload short = 69,000 ('69k = the level to short if reached'). Price is currently BELOW this zone, so this is a sell-the-rally entry, not live.

targets.basis: zero_live 2026-06-22: 1D demand ~61.7 (first downside objective / lows to be swept)
1D demand/accumulation zone on 06-15 charts (~58,823), near production-cost floor (estimated)

safest_plan.stop_basis: A 1H close back above the 12H supply (~66,758, zero_hub charts; zero_tg invalidation = 'close above the 12H supply') negates the bearish rejection.

stance_by_source.summary: Bigger-picture trend down, sellers in control; shorted the 12H supply to a 1H breaker and closed ~7% in profit. Reload short on a flip of ~66.3; short 69k if it gets there; small tactical long 65.8->69 only if conditions allow. Lows being built to be swept. Not a spot idea.
1H short from the 12H supply (~66.8k) down toward the 0.788 fib (~64.3k); the 10x short card shows roughly +28% in profit, avg close ~64,843.
12H supply ~66.8k caps price; 0.788 fib ~64.3k and a demand pocket 64.75-65.25k sit below. Bias to fade the supply.
USDT dominance chart flags a 'confluence for BTC LONG' if dominance drops into the 12H + order-block zone — a counter-condition that would favour a bounce, not the short.
BTC perp marks a long idea off the 6H demand + lower-timeframe breaker around 64.1-64.5k — a tactical buy-the-dip, opposite to the short.
Spot stance: wait / accumulate lower, looking for 60k -> 59k deeper into summer rather than chasing here.
Daily trend bearish, momentum with sellers; production-cost / demand floor sits down near ~58k.
Short triggered at $66,619 off the 12H supply, invalidation a close above the 12H supply; closed in profit, well-managed (10x instrument, context only).

conflicts: The actionable short level (~66.6k 12H supply) is now well above the current price (62,313) — the short already played out and closed in profit, so there is no fresh short to take at current price; it is a sell-the-rally setup only.

Same trader marks both a short (fade the 66.6-69k supply) and a tactical long (buy the 6H demand ~64k, plus a USDT.D 'confluence for BTC long'), so intraday this is a two-sided range, not a clean one-way short.
Spot guidance (wait / accumulate 59-60k for the longer term) sits alongside the tactical short — consistent on direction (down) but different in intent and timeframe.
plain_english: Zero's bigger-picture read on Bitcoin is bearish: the longer-term trend is down and sellers are in control. He already shorted from a resistance zone around $66,600 and closed it in profit as price dropped to about $62,300 today, so there is no fresh short to take right now — price has already fallen below his sell zone. He would only short again if Bitcoin rallies back up into roughly $66,600 to $69,000, and he also flags a possible small bounce-buy near $64,000, so the market is really chopping in a range. Wait for a rally into the $66,600-$69,000 sell zone before shorting; do not chase it lower from here.
